title: Secondary Process Color and Video Mnemonics
breadcrumbs: Secondary Process Color and Video Mnemonics
text: Secondary Process Color and Video Mnemonics The secondary process mnemonics instruct Uniface to perform the video feature in a secondary print or display process, that is, by printing the line a second time. Arguments Table 1. Mnemonics for Secondary Processes Mnemonic Explanation _color Colors for secondary process. _color_n Color combination for index color n , where n is an identifer from 0 through 63. _underscore Video for secondary process. _inverse _high _blink Description The character used for _underscore and _inverse is defined in the character set section of the device translation table. Use these functions for a printer that does not support video attributes directly or for a display that can only perform the functions in graphics mode. If you define any of the color mnemonics (that is, color, color_ n , _color , or _color_ n ), any definition for _inverse is ignored. This also means that printer_inverse in the character set section is ignored. Related Links Uniface Index Color Mnemonics Color mnemonics in the table define the 64 Uniface index color combinations. Video Mode Mnemonics Video mode mnemonics define how the device displays characters in the various video modes and combinations. Secondary Process Character Mnemonics These mnemonics define the character used in a secondary process, for example, for printers which must print a line twice to produce an underline. This technique is also used with some display devices. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
